# Shoonaya

> Multi-tradition spiritual companion for Hindu, Sikh, Buddhist and Jain practice. Covers daily panchang, festivals, vrats, bhakti scripture (stotrams and kathas), Pathshala learning, Japa mala, and daily sadhana tracking.

## What Shoonaya covers

- **Daily Panchang**: Astronomy-backed tithi, nakshatra, yoga, karana, vara, Rahu Kaal, Brahma Muhurta, sunrise/sunset. Uses Lahiri ayanamsha and Ujjain coordinates as the traditional meridian.
- **Festivals & Observances**: 82 observances across 4 traditions. Computed via a declarative rule engine (lunar tithi, solar fixed, nakshatra-based, relative).
- **Vrats (fasting days)**: Ekadashi, Purnima, Amavasya, Pradosh Vrat, Sankashti Chaturthi, Masik Shivaratri, Puranmashi, Uposatha, Vat Savitri Vrat, Vat Savitri Purnima, Hartalika Teej, Karva Chauth, Vaikunta Ekadashi, Navratri, Diwali, Janmashtami, Holi, Gurpurab — with significance, practice instructions, puja items, dos/donts, and mantra.
- **Bhakti Library**: Stotrams (Sanskrit hymns with transliteration and translation), Kathas (sacred stories) across traditions.
- **Pathshala**: Structured learning paths through Bhagavad Gita, Guru Granth Sahib, Dhammapada, Jain Agamas, Ramayana.
- **Japa Mala**: 108-bead digital mala counter with tradition-specific mantras and ambient audio.
- **Daily Sadhana**: Tracks Japa, Nitya Seva (morning routine), Pathshala study, Quiz, Dharm Veer challenge.

## Panchang notes

- Method: Astronomy-backed Panchang estimate
- Precision: Higher precision for daily guidance; observance rules still under validation
- Guidance: This Panchang now uses astronomy-backed solar and lunar positions plus solved transition windows. It is materially stronger than the older in-app estimate, but temple- or guru-specific observances should still win when exact vrata timing matters.

## Festival calendar

The festival dates are calculated using an astronomy-backed rule engine (`src/lib/calendar/engine.ts`, version `1.0.0`). Dates are calculated for the current Gregorian year using Ujjain as the reference meridian (23.1765°N, 75.7885°E).

## Key URLs

- Panchang: https://shoonaya.com/panchang
- Vrats: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/[slug]
- Stotrams: https://shoonaya.com/bhakti/stotram/[id]
- Kathas: https://shoonaya.com/bhakti/katha/[id]
- Pathshala: https://shoonaya.com/pathshala/[pathId]
- Calendar API: https://shoonaya.com/api/calendar/upcoming?days=30
- Daily panchang API: https://shoonaya.com/api/calendar/day?date=YYYY-MM-DD

## Traditions covered

- Hindu (Vaishnava, Shaiva, Shakta perspectives)
- Sikh (Gurmat tradition, Nanakshahi calendar)
- Buddhist (Theravada and Tibetan Mahayana)
- Jain (Shvetambara and Digambara)

- Ekadashi: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/ekadashi
- Purnima: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/purnima

- Pradosh Vrat: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/pradosh
- Sankashti Chaturthi: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/chaturthi
- Masik Shivaratri: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/shivaratri
- Puranmashi: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/puranmashi
- Uposatha: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/uposatha
- Vat Savitri Vrat: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/vat-savitri
- Vat Savitri Purnima: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/vat-savitri-purnima
- Hartalika Teej: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/hartalika-teej
- Karva Chauth: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/karva-chauth
- Vaikunta Ekadashi: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/vaikunta-ekadashi
- Navratri: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/navratri
- Diwali: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/diwali
- Janmashtami: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/janmashtami
- Holi: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/holi
- Gurpurab: https://shoonaya.com/vrat/gurpurab
